title: salty pillowy canadian filo pastry bake
description: a hearty breakfast dish featuring braised lentils and flaky filo pastry in a bamboo-tray. served warm, perfect for post-workout
instructions: preheat oven to 375f. line a bamboo-tray with parchment paper, leaving an overhang for easy removal.
in a large pan over medium heat, sauté the onion, carrots, and celery until the vegetables are tender.
add the mushrooms and cook until they release their liquids and start to brown.
add the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the lentils, beef broth, salt, and black pepper. bring to a simmer and cook until the liquid has thickened and the lentils are tender.
unroll the filo pastry sheets and place them in the bamboo-tray, overlapping the sheets slightly to cover the lentil mixture.
dot the top of the filo pastry with olive oil and sprinkle with feta cheese.
bake for 25 minutes, or until the filo pastry is golden brown and the filling is hot and bubbly.
remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
slice the salad into 6-8 portions and serve warm, garnished with additional feta cheese if desired.
cover and refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 2 months.
reheat in the oven or microwave until hot and crispy.
serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a green salad for a well-rounded meal.
note: you can also make this recipe in a 9x13 inch baking dish. simply adjust the baking time as needed.
note: if using frozen filo pastry, thaw according to package instructions before using.
